---
title: "P.Geo — Professional Geoscientist (Engineers and Geoscientists BC)"
subtitle: "Environmental Geoscience track for the UBCO Freshwater Science degree"
date: last-modified
---

# The designation

**P.Geo** = Professional Geoscientist, granted by
[Engineers and Geoscientists BC (EGBC)](https://www.egbc.ca/). Entry
is **Geoscientist in Training (GIT)** with the academic credential
complete; P.Geo follows after experience and competency assessment.
Exams may be assigned if academic gaps remain.

## Academic requirements (Environmental Geoscience)

EGBC uses a self-evaluation checklist against three pillars:

- **Foundations** — math, chemistry, physics, biology, statistics
- **Compulsory geoscience** — mineralogy / petrology, sedimentology /
  stratigraphy, structural geology, geomorphology, hydrology,
  hydrogeology, geochemistry, geophysics, field techniques
- **Environmental areas** — environmental chemistry, environmental
  geoscience applications

# How Freshwater Science maps to P.Geo

The Freshwater Science core covers the foundations and several
compulsory pillars naturally (hydrology, hydrogeology, geochemistry,
geomorphology, field techniques). The gap to close is the
mineralogy/petrology + sed-strat + structural + geophysics block, plus
intro GIS.

## What's already covered

- **Foundations**: MATH 100/101; CHEM 121/123; PHYS 111/112; BIOL 116.
- **Hydrology**: EESC 205.
- **Geomorphology / soils**: EESC 222.
- **Geochemistry**: EESC 309 / 323 / 423.
- **Environmental chemistry**: CHEM 301.

## Gap-filler courses

To satisfy the compulsory geoscience pillar:

- **Mineralogy / petrology** → **EESC 200 or 201**
- **Sedimentology / stratigraphy** → **EESC 356**
- **Structural geology** → **EESC 325**
- **Field techniques** → **EESC 435**
- **Geophysics (recommended)** → EESC 350
- **Hydrogeology** → **EESC 342**
- **GIS / Remote Sensing** → GEOG / GISC 380, 381

## Sample 4-year plan for the P.Geo track

Illustrative; verify with the program advisor and current EGBC rules.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Year 1
- **T1**: CHEM 121 or 123; MATH 100; PHYS 111 or 112; BIOL 116;
  elective.
- **T2**: The other of CHEM 121/123; MATH 101 or 103; PHYS 112 or 121;
  BIOL 125; elective.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Year 2
- **T1**: EESC 205 (Hydrology); **EESC 200 or 201
  (Mineralogy/Petrology — compulsory)**; STAT 230 or GEOG 271; elective.
- **T2**: EESC 222 (Geomorphology); intro GIS (GEOG/GISC 272/280 if
  offered, or defer to Year 3); CHEM 211; elective.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Year 3
- **T1**: EESC 301 (Limnology); **EESC 325 (Structural — compulsory)**;
  EESC 309 / 323 (Geochemistry); GISC/GEOG 380/381 (GIS/RS).
- **T2**: **EESC 356 (Sed/Strat — compulsory)**; EESC 350 (Geophysics
  — recommended); upper-level elective.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Year 4
- **T1**: **EESC 342 (Hydrogeology)**; EESC 423 (Advanced Geochem
  example); elective.
- **T2**: **EESC 435 (Field Techniques — compulsory/field)**;
  capstone / Directed Studies aligned to environmental geoscience.
:::

## Co-op and experience

Co-op terms after Year 2 into Year 3, targeting environmental
consulting, mining-environment, groundwater, and government hydrology
work. These count toward the GIT post-degree experience that bridges
to P.Geo.

## Seat / prerequisite cautions

- Some EESC 3xx and 4xx courses require specific lower-division
  geoscience prerequisites — take **EESC 200/201 early** to keep the
  upper-level path open.
- GIS / Remote Sensing courses (GISC 380/381) have priority rules for
  majors. Request permission early.

# Your gap-finder (interactive)

::: {.callout-tip}
## When the planner is wired up
The interactive planner cross-checks your completed-courses list
against the EGBC environmental-geoscience pillars (foundations,
compulsory geoscience, environmental areas) and flags which
compulsory courses you have versus still need.
:::

# References

- [Engineers and Geoscientists BC — Become a Member](https://www.egbc.ca/Become-a-Member)
- [EGBC Environmental Geoscience Syllabus](https://www.egbc.ca/) — locate the geoscience syllabi PDF in the EGBC publications portal.
- Source notes in this repo: `docs/reference/advising/UBCO_Freshwater_Professional_Paths_Advising_Pack.pdf`.
